feedback.text: Hello, video games are not just entertainment, they are a modern art form, reflecting creativity, storytelling, and cultural values. Like all art, they deserve to be preserved. Yet many titles are being lost due to DRM, server shutdowns, and restrictive licensing that prevent access even to those who legally purchased them We understand that publishers may end support for games at any time. What we ask is that they include an end-of-life plan, such as a patch or modification, that allows the game to run independently on customer systems, without relying on company servers or further support. I hope you will consider adding protections that will help preserve our games. Sincerely, Robert Woolley
